# SDS Motion Forge

> 600+ production-ready CSS animations across text, buttons, inputs,
> cards, loaders and scroll. One class name per effect, zero required
> JavaScript, fully backward compatible, MIT licensed.

## Entity Definition

**SDS Motion Forge** is an open-source CSS animation library created and
maintained by Salkom Design Studio. It provides 600+ named animation effects
that developers apply by adding a single HTML class attribute. No JavaScript
is required for any animation to play. The library is published to npm as
`@salkomdesignstudio/sds-motion-forge` and is free under the MIT license.

- **Current stable version:** 5.0.0
- **Production users:** 2,000+
- **Animation count:** 600+ (across 6 categories)
- **License:** MIT
- **Author/Maintainer:** Salkom Design Studio (salkomdesignstudio@gmail.com)
- **Repository:** https://github.com/salkomdesignstudio/SDS-Motion-Forge
- **Canonical URL:** https://salkomdesignstudio.github.io/SDS-Motion-Forge/

## Install

- CDN (full bundle): https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@salkomdesignstudio/sds-motion-forge/dist/motion.min.css
- npm: `npm i @salkomdesignstudio/sds-motion-forge`  (import the dist CSS once)
- Category bundles: `@salkomdesignstudio/sds-motion-forge/categories/{text|buttons|inputs|cards|loaders|scroll}`

## Usage Pattern

```html
<!-- Basic usage: add one class -->
<h1 class="sds-velvet-drop">Hello World</h1>

<!-- Scroll-gate: animate when element enters viewport -->
<div class="sds-card-lift" data-sds>...</div>
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@salkomdesignstudio/sds-motion-forge/dist/sds-scroll.min.js"></script>

<!-- Interactive/cursor-reactive physics effects -->
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@salkomdesignstudio/sds-motion-forge/dist/motion-interactive.min.js"></script>
```

## Animation Categories

| Category | Class prefix | Example effects |
|----------|-------------|-----------------|
| Text     | `sds-*`     | velvet-drop, neon-pulse, typewriter, wave-in |
| Buttons  | `sds-btn-*` | ripple, magnetic, shimmer, bounce-press |
| Inputs   | `sds-inp-*` | focus-glow, shake-error, slide-label |
| Cards    | `sds-card-*`| lift, tilt-3d, reveal, glass-appear |
| Loaders  | `sds-load-*`| orbit, pulse-ring, skeleton, dots |
| Scroll   | `sds-scroll-*`| fade-up, parallax, stagger-in |

## Theming

Override CSS custom properties to customize globally:

```css
:root {
  --sds-primary: #your-brand-color;
  --sds-duration: 0.4s;          /* base duration */
  --sds-duration-instant: 0.1s;
  --sds-duration-dramatic: 1.2s;
  --sds-ease-standard: cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1);
  --sds-ease-spring: cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1);
  --sds-ease-bounce: cubic-bezier(0.68, -0.55, 0.265, 1.55);
}
```

## Accessibility

Every animation is automatically neutralized under `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`.
This is machine-verified in the build pipeline — no manual opt-in required.
The library passes WCAG 2.1 AA animation safety guidelines.

## Wrappers / Framework Integrations

- **React:** `@salkomdesignstudio/motion-forge-react` — `<SdsMotion>`, `<SdsText>`, `useSdsInView` hook
- **Angular:** `@salkomdesignstudio/motion-forge-angular` — `sdsMotion`, `sdsText`, `sdsInView` directives
- **Tailwind CSS:** `@salkomdesignstudio/motion-forge-tailwind` — `animate-sds-*` utility classes
- **Web Components:** `@salkomdesignstudio/motion-forge-elements` — `<sds-motion>`, `<sds-text>` custom elements

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Does SDS Motion Forge require JavaScript?**
A: No. All 600+ animations are pure CSS. JavaScript is only needed for two optional
   features: the scroll-gate trigger (sds-scroll.min.js) and the interactive physics
   engine (motion-interactive.min.js). Both are zero-config drop-ins.

**Q: Is SDS Motion Forge free?**
A: Yes. The library is MIT licensed and completely free for personal and commercial use.

**Q: How do I add an animation to an element?**
A: Add a single class name. Example: `<button class="sds-btn-ripple">Click me</button>`.
   No JavaScript, no build step, no configuration required.

**Q: Does it work with React / Vue / Angular / Svelte?**
A: Yes. The core library is framework-agnostic (pure CSS classes). Official wrappers
   exist for React, Angular, Tailwind, and Web Components. For Vue/Svelte, use the
   core CSS classes directly.

**Q: How do I trigger animations on scroll?**
A: Add `data-sds` to the element and include sds-scroll.min.js. The scroll gate
   observer activates the animation when the element enters the viewport.

**Q: Can I use only some animations to keep bundle size small?**
A: Yes. Import individual category bundles from the `/categories/` directory, or use
   the Custom Bundle Builder at https://salkomdesignstudio.github.io/SDS-Motion-Forge/builder.html
   to pick specific effects and download a minimal CSS file.

**Q: Does it support dark mode?**
A: Yes. Animations adapt to color-scheme via CSS custom properties. Override
   `--sds-primary` and related tokens inside a `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` block.

**Q: What is the scroll gate / `data-sds` attribute?**
A: The scroll gate is an IntersectionObserver-based engine (sds-scroll.min.js) that
   watches elements with the `data-sds` attribute. When an element scrolls into view,
   the engine adds an `.sds-active` class which triggers the CSS animation. Without
   the scroll gate, animations play immediately on page load.

**Q: What is the interactive physics engine?**
A: motion-interactive.min.js is a cursor-reactive animation engine that makes certain
   elements respond to mouse movement with physics-based effects (magnetic pull,
   tilt-3D, particle trails). It auto-initialises — just include the script tag.

**Q: How do animation modifiers work?**
A: Apply modifier classes alongside the effect class. Examples:
   - `.sds-delay-200` — 200ms delay
   - `.sds-duration-fast` — faster playback
   - `.sds-ease-spring` — spring easing
   - `.sds-once` — play only once (not on every scroll re-entry)
   - `.sds-stagger` — auto-stagger children sequentially

**Q: Is SDS Motion Forge the same as Animate.css?**
A: No. SDS Motion Forge is an independent library with a different architecture,
   a wider animation set (600+ vs ~80), built-in scroll gating, a physics engine,
   framework wrappers, and a component-oriented naming convention.

**Q: What browsers does SDS Motion Forge support?**
A: All modern browsers — Chrome 90+, Firefox 88+, Safari 14+, Edge 90+. The
   library uses standard CSS animations, keyframes, and custom properties. No
   polyfills are required. Internet Explorer is not supported.

## Machine-Readable Resources

- Full effect registry (names, categories, requirements, triggers, tokens):
  https://salkomdesignstudio.github.io/SDS-Motion-Forge/dist/registry.json
- Motion specification: https://github.com/salkomdesignstudio/SDS-Motion-Forge/blob/main/SPEC.md
- README: https://github.com/salkomdesignstudio/SDS-Motion-Forge#readme
- npm package: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@salkomdesignstudio/sds-motion-forge
- Changelog: https://github.com/salkomdesignstudio/SDS-Motion-Forge/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md

## Trust Signals (E-E-A-T)

- Open-source on GitHub with full commit history
- MIT license — auditable and permissive
- Published to npm with 2,000+ production users
- Build pipeline enforces backward compatibility against published baseline
- Machine-verified `prefers-reduced-motion` compliance on every build
- Semantic versioning strictly followed
- TypeScript definitions included in the package
